evals.prompt: I want to store cusps for a Bianchi group in the database. Each cusp has a label (order_label.cusp_hash), an order (text), a representative (jsonb — a Clifford algebra element stored as [num_list, den]), a stabilizer_rank (integer), and timing (text). The label is the order label followed by the base-62 hash of the representative. Store one file per order at DATA/cusps/<order_label>. No caching needed yet — file a GitHub issue for that. Also file an issue for the make script.
evals.expected_output: Type declaration + ATTRS (alphabetical, Sort), Print, load_lmfdb_cusp, eq, get_lmfdb_cusp_path (MonStgElt + CliffOrd + GrpPSL2Cliff overloads), read_lmfdb_cusps, write_lmfdb_cusp, create_lmfdb_cusp scaffold. package-IO.mag gets cusp_hash in TextCols, stabilizer_rank in IntegerCols, representative in JsonbCols. schema.md gets a Cusps table. Two GitHub issues filed.

assertions.name: ATTRS_is_sorted
assertions.description: LMFDBCusp_ATTRS uses Sort([...]) and has //ALWAYS ALPHABETICAL! comment

assertions.name: label_in_TextCols
assertions.description: label attribute is registered in TextCols in package-IO.mag

assertions.name: no_get_label_SL2O
assertions.description: No call to get_label(SL2O) without a second argument anywhere in the generated code

assertions.name: no_sig_files_touched
assertions.description: No .sig files are created or modified

assertions.name: schema_table_present
assertions.description: schema.md has a new ## Cusps section with a column table

assertions.name: SetColumns_in_path
assertions.description: SetColumns(0) appears in the path function

evals.prompt: Add a new LMFDB type LMFDBTorsionElement. Attributes: label (text, scheme: order_label.base62_hash_of_matrix), order (text), matrix (jsonb — a 2x2 Clifford matrix), order_of_element (integer), word_in_fp_group (jsonb — integer sequence), timing (text). One file per entry: DATA/torsion/<order_label>/<entry_label>. The populating function is compute_torsion_elements(SL2O) which returns a list of matrices. Cache on GrpPSL2Cliff as torsion_cache (already declared). Write the make script for Gaussian integers now.
evals.expected_output: Full type scaffold plus make/make-torsion-1.mag that loads SL2O, calls compute_torsion_elements, checks per-file existence with OpenTest, creates and writes each entry. Per-entry path functions (two overloads). torsion_cache population in load intrinsic.

assertions.name: per_entry_path
assertions.description: Path function takes (SL2O, entry_label) and produces DATA/torsion/<order_label>/<entry_label>

assertions.name: make_script_restartable
assertions.description: make-torsion-1.mag checks OpenTest for each file before computing (restartable)

assertions.name: ATTRS_alphabetical
assertions.description: LMFDBTorsionElement_ATTRS = Sort([...]) with all attribute names present

assertions.name: read_returns_single
assertions.description: read_lmfdb_torsion_element(fname) returns a single object, not a SetIndx, with assert #lines eq 1

evals.prompt: I need to store Hecke eigenvalue data. Type name: LMFDBHeckeEigenvalue. Attributes: label (text, scheme: gamma0_label.eigenvalue_index where eigenvalue_index is an integer formatted as a string), gamma0_label (text), eigenvalue (jsonb — a rational number stored as [num, den]), hecke_matrix_label (text), index (integer), timing (text). Path: one file per gamma0 group at DATA/hecke_eigenvalues/<order_label>/<gamma0_label>. No make script yet — file an issue. No caching needed.
evals.expected_output: Full type scaffold. Path takes (SL2O, gamma0_label) overload. gamma0_label in TextCols, hecke_matrix_label in TextCols, index in IntegerCols, eigenvalue in JsonbCols. schema.md table. GitHub issue for make script.

assertions.name: gamma0_label_in_TextCols
assertions.description: gamma0_label registered in TextCols in package-IO.mag

assertions.name: label_scheme_documented
assertions.description: schema.md table documents the label scheme: gamma0_label.N
